# Oracle Memory — File-backed Memory MCP Server

## What

Oracle Memory is an MCP server that gives AI coding agents persistent memory across sessions.
No database, no external service — just JSON files under `.oracle-memory/`.

## How to use

### Start the server
```bash
npx tsx src/index.ts
```

### Register in Claude Code
```bash
claude mcp add oracle-memory -- node /path/to/oracle-memory/dist/index.js
```

### Register in Codex
Add to Codex MCP config pointing to the same path.

## Memory Types
| Type | Purpose | Lifetime |
|------|---------|---------|
| fact | Permanent knowledge | Forever |
| insight | Lessons learned, gotchas | Forever |
| chunk | Conversation snapshots | TTL-based |
| working | Scratchpad | Session only |

## MCP Tools

### remember(agent, type, content, tags?, source?, importance?, ttl?)
Save a memory. Type must be one of: fact, insight, chunk, working.

### recall(query, agent?, type?, tags?, limit?)
Search across all memories. Uses hybrid BM25 + vector + entity graph ranking.

### get_memory(id, type)
Retrieve a single memory by ID and type.

### update_memory(id, type, content?, tags?, importance?, meta?, ttl?)
Update mutable fields of an existing memory. Re-indexes vector + entity graph.

### list_memories(type?, agent?, tags?, limit?, query?)
List/filter memories with optional free-text query.

### forget(id, type)
Permanently delete a memory by ID and type.

### clear_working(agent?)
Clear working memory for an agent (or all).

### consolidate()
Merge similar memories by tag overlap into consolidated entries.

### get_stats()
Get memory counts by type and agent.

### get_sessions()
List currently connected agent sessions.

## MCP Resources
- `oracle-memory://memories` — all memories (newest first, max 200)
- `oracle-memory://memories/{type}` — by type
- `oracle-memory://stats` — statistics
- `oracle-memory://sessions` — active sessions

## HTTP endpoints
| Path | Method | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `/mcp` | POST/GET | MCP protocol endpoint |
| `/health` | GET | Health check (`{"status":"ok","uptime":123}`) |

## Configuration
| Env | Default | Description |
|-----|---------|-------------|
| ORACLE_MEMORY_ROOT_DIR | cwd | Data directory |
| ORACLE_MEMORY_DISABLE_VECTORS | false | Set "1" to disable |
| ORACLE_MEMORY_TRANSPORT | stdio | stdio or http/streamable |
| ORACLE_MEMORY_PORT | 8765 | HTTP port |
| ORACLE_MEMORY_HOST | 0.0.0.0 | HTTP bind |
| ORACLE_MEMORY_HTTP_TOKEN | — | Bearer auth for HTTP |
| ORACLE_MEMORY_LOG_LEVEL | info | debug/info/warn/error |

## On-disk layout
```
.oracle-memory/
├── config.json
├── facts/
├── insights/
├── chunks/
├── working/
├── graph/graph.json
└── vectors/ (optional)
```

## Example workflow
```
→ remember("claude", "fact", "Project uses port 3000", ["config"])
→ recall("port configuration")
← [{ entry: { content: "Project uses port 3000" }, score: 2.08, method: "bm25" }]
→ update_memory("20260713-...", "fact", { content: "Project uses port 4000" })
→ get_stats()
← { totalMemories: 42, byType: { fact: 20, insight: 10, chunk: 10, working: 2 } }
```

## Build & test
```bash
npm run build        # tsc → dist/
npm run check        # type-check only
npm test             # vitest (20 tests)
npm run dev          # tsx (dev mode)
```
